question_text: A student uses a food calorimeter to measure the energy released when 2 grams of peanuts are burned. The calorimeter heats 100 cm³ of water from 20°C to 28°C. How much energy in joules was released by the peanuts? (Use: 4.2 J needed to raise 1 gram of water by 1°C)
explanation: Energy = mass × specific heat capacity × temperature change. Mass of water = 100 cm³ = 100 grams. Temperature change = 28°C - 20°C = 8°C. Energy = 100 × 4.2 × 8 = 3360 J. This represents the chemical energy stored in the peanuts that was released during burning.
